---
title: "Solution Overview"
format: html
---

### Concert Operate for Batch Processing: The What

IBM Concert Operate is an AIOps platform deployed on OpenShift Container Platform (OCP)
that provides event correlation, noise reduction, and runbook automation. In this solution,
it is positioned as the intelligence layer between a batch job event source and an ITSM
ticketing system.

The core capability applied here is **temporal and scope-based event correlation**: Concert
Operate groups related events (those that occur within a defined time window and match a
defined scope: job name, job group, or dependency chain) into a single correlated incident.
Only one ticket is raised for the correlated group, suppressing the duplicate and cascential
noise that would otherwise flood the queue.

**Key capabilities used in this solution:**

- **Temporal correlation** ("x events in y minutes"): groups repeated alerts from the same
  source into a single incident once a persistence threshold is crossed
- **Scope-based grouping:** correlates events across a job dependency chain into one incident
- **Deduplication:** suppresses duplicate tickets for the same underlying failure
- **Runbook automation:** triggers a defined automated response (e.g. job retry) when a
  correlation policy fires

**Why Concert Operate over alternatives:**

The solution is deployed self-hosted on OCP, which fits environments with strict data
residency or air-gap requirements. It integrates with existing event forwarding mechanisms
without requiring changes to the batch scheduler or monitoring toolchain. Only the outbound
event destination is retargeted.
